org_constraint: The word "resign" must appear exactly three times, conveying the definition "to voluntarily leave a job or position."
new_constraint: Avoid using the keyword "resign" in the email.

Sincerely,

I must inform you that your instructions contain a direct contradiction: you requested the word "resign" appear exactly three times, but simultaneously instructed to avoid using it in the email. It is impossible to satisfy both conditions simultaneously within the same text.

To resolve this, I have prioritized your negative constraint (avoiding the word) over the positive counting constraint. Below is the email drafted without the word "resign".

***

**Subject:** Notice of Employment Termination – [Your Name]

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this letter as formal notification that I will be leaving my position at [Company Name]. My last day will be [Last Date].

This has been a challenging but rewarding time working with our team. I appreciate the opportunities you gave me during my tenure here.

I wish you and the company the best for the future. Please let me know how I can facilitate a smooth handover of my current projects before my departure.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>
